corpus_meta.name: FSOS Compliance Intelligence — Seed Knowledge Corpus
corpus_meta.purpose: Grounding corpus for the NIGO analysis + note-authoring engine. Every chunk is tagged with authority_type (the tier hierarchy) and section_ref (for precise citation). Load into knowledge_chunks; embed chunk_text.
corpus_meta.verification_note: FINRA/SEC/state text below is paraphrased-for-index with section_refs to the authoritative source. BEFORE any chunk is used to ground an external NIGO response, load the verbatim rule text from the primary source and set verbatim=true. Chunks are marked verbatim=false until you do.
corpus_meta.critical_gap: FFS_PROCEDURE and CARRIER_REQUIREMENT chunks are STUBS. The system cannot cite an FFS manual or carrier rule it has never seen. You must upload the actual FFS compliance manual, WSPs, FCB bulletins, and carrier submission guides to populate these tiers.

chunks.title: 2330 — Reasonable basis to believe suitability (disclosure of features)
chunks.chunk_text: Before recommending the purchase or exchange of a deferred variable annuity, the associated person must have a reasonable basis to believe the customer has been informed of the material features of the annuity, including surrender charges, potential tax penalties, mortality and expense fees, investment advisory fees, potential charges for and features of riders, insurance and investment components, and market risk.

chunks.title: 2330 — Exchange-specific suitability factors
chunks.chunk_text: For an exchange of a deferred variable annuity, the associated person must consider whether: the customer would incur a surrender charge, be subject to a new surrender period, lose existing benefits (such as death, living, or other contractual benefits), or be subject to increased fees or charges; the customer would benefit from product enhancements and improvements; and the customer has had another exchange within the preceding 36 months.
governs_nigo_patterns: loss of existing benefits not documented

chunks.title: 2330 — Suitability profile factors that must be considered
chunks.chunk_text: The recommendation must be suitable considering the customer's age, annual income, financial situation and needs, investment experience, investment objectives, intended use of the deferred variable annuity, investment time horizon, existing assets, liquidity needs, liquid net worth, risk tolerance, tax status, and such other information used or considered reasonable.

chunks.title: 2330 — Principal review standard and trigger
chunks.chunk_text: Prior to transmitting a customer's application for a deferred variable annuity to the issuing insurance company, a registered principal must review and determine whether he or she approves of the recommended transaction. The principal reviews within seven business days of the firm receiving a COMPLETE and CORRECT application package. The trigger is a complete and correct package; the seven business days is a ceiling, not a floor.

chunks.title: Reg BI — Care Obligation
chunks.chunk_text: The Care Obligation requires that the broker, in making a recommendation, exercise reasonable diligence, care, and skill to: (A) understand potential risks, rewards, and costs of the recommendation and have a reasonable basis to believe it could be in the retail customer's best interest; (B) have a reasonable basis to believe the recommendation is in the best interest of the particular retail customer based on that customer's investment profile; and (C) have a reasonable basis to believe that a series of recommended transactions is not excessive. The best-interest basis must not place the broker's interest ahead of the customer's.

chunks.title: Reg BI — Reasonably available alternatives
chunks.chunk_text: As part of the Care Obligation, the broker should consider reasonably available alternatives offered by the firm as part of having a reasonable basis to believe the recommendation is in the customer's best interest. The broker need not evaluate every possible alternative, nor recommend the single 'best' product, but must consider reasonable alternatives and should be able to explain why the recommendation was selected over them.
governs_nigo_patterns: single fund family vs multi family

chunks.title: 2111 — Suitability
chunks.chunk_text: A member or associated person must have a reasonable basis to believe that a recommended transaction or investment strategy is suitable for the customer, based on the information obtained through reasonable diligence to ascertain the customer's investment profile, including age, other investments, financial situation and needs, tax status, investment objectives, investment experience, time horizon, liquidity needs, and risk tolerance.

chunks.section_ref: Rule 2342 / NTM 95-80 lineage; mutual fund breakpoint guidance
chunks.title: Breakpoints / Rights of Accumulation / Letter of Intent
chunks.chunk_text: Breakpoint discounts reduce front-end sales charges as investment size increases within a single fund family, via Rights of Accumulation (aggregating related holdings) and Letters of Intent. The regulatory concern is breakpoint SELLING abuse: structuring transactions to DEPRIVE a customer of a breakpoint discount they would otherwise qualify for, typically to preserve or increase commission. The rules do NOT prohibit investing across multiple fund families when the customer is informed of and understands the forgone breakpoint discount and the multi-family structure serves the customer's documented objectives.
chunks.leverage_note: This chunk is the direct bounce for the 'one fund family or decline' NIGO. The rule protects the client's discount; it does not mandate consolidation. A blanket 'one family or decline' is not supported by this FINRA rule — it is at most FFS_PROCEDURE. Retrieval here should surface the gap.

chunks.title: 2010 — Standards of Commercial Honor
chunks.chunk_text: A member, in the conduct of its business, shall observe high standards of commercial honor and just and equitable principles of trade. This rule underlies enforcement of signature integrity: forging or falsifying a customer signature, including via electronic signature platforms, violates Rule 2010 and does not require intent to defraud.

chunks.title: 4511 — Books and Records
chunks.chunk_text: Members must make and preserve books and records as required under FINRA rules, the Exchange Act, and applicable Exchange Act rules. Record integrity — including accurate, non-altered documentation and proper e-signature audit trails — falls under this obligation together with Rule 2010.

chunks.title: RN 22-18 — Digital signature forgery and falsification
chunks.chunk_text: FINRA reminded firms of obligations to supervise for forgery and falsification of signatures via third-party digital signature platforms (Rules 2010, 4511, 3110). Red flags firms identified include: customer signatures originating from an email address associated with the representative; a customer signing IP address matching the representative's or inconsistent with the customer's address on file; documents sent to a representative-associated or outside-business email. Such conduct violates Rule 2010 and does not require intent to defraud.
chunks.leverage_note: HIGHEST PERSONAL RISK. Enforcement, not just NIGO. If a case file shows a client signing from a rep-domain email, flag it as enforcement risk and route to compliance principal — do not treat as a routine correction.

chunks.section_ref: 2024 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report — Variable Annuities, Effective Practices
chunks.title: 2024 Report — Detailed, non-replicated exchange rationales
chunks.chunk_text: Effective practice: confirm that registered representatives' — and where applicable supervisory principals' — written rationales for variable annuity exchanges address the specific circumstances FOR EACH CUSTOMER and do NOT replicate rationales provided for other customers; and require supervisory principals to VERIFY the information in these rationales, including product fees, costs, rider benefits, and existing product values. Data considered in exchange review includes: living/death benefit value forfeited, surrender charges incurred, source of funds, share class, riders, issuer and purchase date of the exchanged policy, and any prior exchange within 36 months.
governs_nigo_patterns: why the customer desires this feature
chunks.leverage_note: This is the objective standard behind most 'not good enough' NIGOs. It is finite and listable. A note that hits every element here satisfies the standard; a reviewer who NIGOs it anyway must name the specific missing element.

chunks.section_ref: 2024 Annual Regulatory Oversight Report — Variable Annuities, Findings
chunks.title: 2024 Report — Data quality / integrity findings
chunks.chunk_text: Finding: poor and insufficient data quality — failing to collect and retain key VA transaction information and failing to address inconsistencies in available data, data formats, and reporting. Effective practice: confirm data integrity with carriers and third-party providers and address inconsistencies. This grounds the requirement that a suitability tool's answers (e.g., RightBridge fields) be internally consistent with the case notes and other documents.

chunks.section_ref: TX 28 TAC §§3.9701 et seq. (replacement); Ins. Code Ch. 1115
chunks.title: Texas — Replacement of life insurance and annuities
chunks.chunk_text: Texas replacement regulations require specific replacement notices and disclosures when an existing life insurance policy or annuity is replaced. Required replacement forms and the associated signatures are non-waivable. Texas Insurance Code §1115.054 provides a safe harbor recognizing compliance with FINRA suitability rules for certain annuity transactions, but does not waive the required state replacement forms.

chunks.section_ref: NAIC Model #613 — Replacement of Life Insurance and Annuities
chunks.title: NAIC #613 — Replacement notice signature/timing (absolute)
chunks.chunk_text: The replacement model requires the producer to present and read (or have the applicant read) the required replacement notice, and to obtain the applicant's and producer's signatures on the notice at or before the time the application is taken. This signature and timing requirement is absolute and not curable by later administrative amendment.

chunks.title: 1035 exchange — loan carryover / boot
chunks.chunk_text: A §1035 exchange is tax-free only to the extent no 'boot' is received. If an outstanding policy loan is extinguished at surrender rather than carried over to the receiving contract, the debt relief is treated as boot — a distribution taxable as ordinary income to the extent of gain. Therefore, on any 1035 with an outstanding loan, written confirmation from the ceding carrier that the loan will carry over is required to support the tax-free characterization before the exchange is approved.

chunks.section_ref: FFS Reg BI Supplement 326349 (structured/buffered VA description)
chunks.title: Buffered/structured VA — limited protection + caps
chunks.chunk_text: A structured/buffered variable annuity offers LIMITED downside protection, not full principal protection: the insurer absorbs the first portion of an annual loss (e.g., the first 10%) and the owner bears losses beyond that; these contracts also cap upside growth in a given period. Exiting a segment before maturity triggers an Interim Value Adjustment (IVA) that may be positive or negative and can reduce the value available for transfer. Notes must describe protection as limited (never full principal protection) and, where an IVA applies to the transaction, disclose it specifically.

chunks.title: Form 311883 — eSignature Terms & Conditions (DocuSign outside Docupace)
chunks.chunk_text: When DocuSign is used outside of Docupace, the completed eSignature Terms and Conditions Form (311883) must be included with the submission. A DocuSign certificate of completion does NOT satisfy this requirement and is not a substitute for the signed form.
chunks.stub_note: Confirmed from a real NIGO. Upload the actual 311883 form instructions to make this verbatim.

chunks.title: FFS internal procedures — PLACEHOLDER
chunks.chunk_text: PLACEHOLDER. The system cannot cite FFS-specific procedures it has not ingested. Upload the FFS compliance manual, WSPs, FCB bulletins, and Docupace submission procedures. Until then, any NIGO requirement that maps ONLY to an FFS procedure (not a FINRA/SEC/state rule) must be flagged as 'firm policy — not verifiable against uploaded FFS documents,' which is itself the correct and honest output.
chunks.stub_note: This is the single most valuable tier to populate. It is what lets the system say 'this is FFS policy, not FINRA' with a citation.

chunks.title: Carrier submission requirements — PLACEHOLDER
chunks.chunk_text: PLACEHOLDER per carrier (Pacific Life, Athene, MassMutual, Equitable, FNWL, etc.). Upload each carrier's submission and form requirements. Example real requirement already observed: Pacific Life accepts a 1035 exchange with an outstanding loan only on the condition that the ceding carrier permits the loan to carry over.
chunks.stub_note: Upload carrier submission guides to populate.
